commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR reverses Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite normalization so preview IDs map to the GA ID, updates Google provider aliases/templates/default aliases, CLI aliasing, and related tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +18. Total +24 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and v2026.5.22 map `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` to `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, and the linked issue reports the same `/model` behavior. I did not run a live Google API call in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining issues are narrow docs/manifest guidance updates that an automated repair can make without choosing a new product direction.

target.title: fix(google): stop appending -preview to gemini-3.1-flash-lite
target.body:
## Summary

Google graduated `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` to GA on 2026-05-07 and deprecated the `-preview` endpoint (shutdown 2026-05-25). OpenClaw's normalizer unconditionally appends `-preview` to every `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` reference, sending requests to a deprecated endpoint and ignoring the user's explicit model choice.

This reverses the mapping direction: the non-preview GA name is now canonical, and old `-preview` references normalize to the GA id.

Closes #86151

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/google/model-id.ts` | Reverse normalizer: `flash-lite-preview` maps to `flash-lite` (was the opposite) |
| `extensions/google/openclaw.plugin.json` | Reverse alias key/value in all three provider sections |
| `src/plugin-sdk/provider-model-id-normalize.ts` | Same reversal in the shared SDK normalizer |
| `src/config/defaults.ts` | `gemini-flash-lite` alias now points to GA id |

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` no longer gets `-preview` appended by the normalizer; the GA model id is preserved as-is

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24.1.0, direct runtime import of both normalizers from source

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
node --input-type=module -e '
  import { normalizeGooglePreviewModelId } from "./src/plugin-sdk/provider-model-id-normalize.ts";
  console.log("SDK:", normalizeGooglePreviewModelId("gemini-3.1-flash-lite"));
  console.log("SDK preview:", normalizeGooglePreviewModelId("gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview"));
'
node --input-type=module -e '
  import { normalizeGoogleModelId } from "./extensions/google/model-id.ts";
  console.log("Extension:", normalizeGoogleModelId("gemini-3.1-flash-lite"));
  console.log("Extension preview:", normalizeGoogleModelId("gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview"));
'
```

Evidence after fix:
```
$ node --input-type=module -e 'import { normalizeGooglePreviewModelId } from "./src/plugin-sdk/provider-model-id-normalize.ts"; ...'
SDK: gemini-3.1-flash-lite
SDK preview: gemini-3.1-flash-lite

$ node --input-type=module -e 'import { normalizeGoogleModelId } from "./extensions/google/model-id.ts"; ...'
Extension: gemini-3.1-flash-lite
Extension preview: gemini-3.1-flash-lite
```
Before this patch both normalizers returned `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` for the `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` input.

Observed result after fix: both `normalizeGooglePreviewModelId("gemini-3.1-flash-lite")` and `normalizeGoogleModelId("gemini-3.1-flash-lite")` return `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` (the GA id, no `-preview` suffix appended). The deprecated `-preview` variant also normalizes to the GA id.

What was not tested: live Google Gemini API call (no API credentials in the test environment)
target.author: sebtardif

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission none are not allowed
